summary:
The best approach involves using a combination of radioactively labeled water and albumin as indicators during an intravascular injection procedure to measure changes indicative of total extracellular space alterations reliably.
To further refine this summary based directly from Agent1’s perspective after considering additional information about different measurement techniques while focusing strictly on two core sentences that capture essential aspects related specifically back to identifying how one might determine I.F.V., it would read thusly:

The most accurate technique uses dual-labeled tracers—radioactive water combined with radiolabled albumin—to assess precise shifts within compartments reflective of true interstitium dynamics post-injection via IV route; other single-tracer approaches like ion-specific probes offer less comprehensive coverage compared to multi-component strategies ensuring more reliable quantification outcomes necessary clinically speaking when evaluating such parameters critically important across various medical conditions requiring careful hydration management among others where exacting precision matters significantly due its direct impact upon patient care decisions involving electrolyte balance adjustments etc... To ensure clarity without exceeding brevity constraints hereafter presented concise yet informative statement reflecting primary intent remains unchanged but slightly adjusted wording improves readability & conciseness maintaining original meaning intact :

Combining radioactively tagged water alongside albuimin provides superior accuracy over singular tracer alternatives allowing clinicians critical insight needed especially regarding complex cases necessitating meticulous monitoring adjusting treatment plans accordingly thereby enhancing overall quality-of-care delivered efficiently effectively addressing initial query posed concerning optimal means assessing IFV . 

---
